title: Context switching is killing my productivity
text: As a PM, I spend 6+ hours in meetings daily. By the time I get to actual work like writing PRDs or analyzing data, my brain is fried. The constant context switching between strategic discussions, tactical execution, and stakeholder management is exhausting. I feel like I never have time for deep work.

title: Stakeholder alignment nightmare
text: Engineering wants to refactor, Sales wants new features yesterday, Marketing wants a completely different roadmap, and Leadership keeps changing priorities. I spend 80% of my time just trying to get everyone aligned. It's like herding cats. I need a tool that helps me visualize trade-offs and get buy-in faster.

title: Why PRDs are broken and what to do about it
text: Traditional PRDs take 20+ hours to write, become outdated within weeks, and nobody reads them anyway. PMs are stuck in documentation hell while the product moves forward. We need living documents that evolve with the product, not static PDFs that no one references.

title: PM tools are fragmented and don't talk to each other
text: I use Notion for docs, Linear for tickets, Figma for designs, Slack for communication, Confluence for wikis, and spreadsheets for roadmaps. Nothing integrates well. I spend hours copy-pasting between tools. Would pay good money for a unified PM workspace.

title: State of Product Management 2024: Top Challenges
text: Survey of 2000+ PMs reveals top challenges: 1) Getting stakeholder alignment (67%), 2) Prioritization with limited resources (58%), 3) Keeping documentation updated (52%), 4) Communicating product value (48%), 5) Balancing strategic vs tactical work (45%).

title: PM burnout is real
text: Been a PM for 5 years. The job has become increasingly about managing expectations and less about building great products. Between endless Jira tickets, stakeholder meetings, and executive presentations, I barely have time to talk to users. Considering a career change.

title: The hidden cost of poor product discovery
text: Most PMs spend less than 10% of their time on customer discovery. We're too busy managing backlogs and stakeholder requests. But then we build features nobody wants. The cycle repeats. We need better tools for continuous user research that don't require 40 hours per week.

title: Feature factory syndrome
text: My company treats product as a feature factory. No time for strategy, just ship features. Roadmap is dictated by sales deals. I became a glorified project manager. Missing the days when I could actually think about user problems and innovation.

title: Data overload but insight starvation
text: We have 50+ dashboards but I still can't answer basic questions quickly. Analytics tools are too complex, data is siloed, and by the time I get insights, the sprint is over. PMs need better self-serve analytics that surface actionable insights automatically.

title: Why I'm frustrated with existing PM tools
text: Tried every PM tool out there. Jira is too complex for stakeholders. Notion is too flexible and becomes messy. Linear is great for eng but lacks PM-specific features. Coda is promising but performance issues. There's no perfect tool for the PM workflow.

title: 产品经理的沟通困境：如何让研发听懂你的需求
text: 作为产品经理，最痛苦的不是写文档，而是让研发团队理解业务价值。需求文档写了几十页，开发还是做出来的和预期不一样。来回沟通浪费大量时间。需要更好的方式来对齐技术团队和业务目标。

title: 产品经理的一天都在做什么？为什么感觉一直在开会
text: 做了三年产品经理，感觉每天80%的时间都在开会。早会、需求评审、技术评审、设计评审、周报会、月度复盘。真正思考产品的时间少之又少。这种状态正常吗？大家都是怎么管理时间的？

title: The death of the traditional PRD
text: Nobody reads 30-page PRDs anymore. Engineers want user stories, designers want prototypes, stakeholders want presentations. PMs are creating the same content in 5 different formats. We need a single source of truth that outputs to multiple formats automatically.

title: Roadmap planning is a nightmare
text: Every quarter it's the same struggle. Leadership wants a 12-month roadmap. Engineering wants to know what's next sprint. Sales wants commitments for deals. Marketing wants launch dates. And I'm supposed to make all this work with dependencies I can't control. Roadmap tools are either too rigid or too flexible.

title: PM salary doesn't match the stress
text: PM role has become one of the most stressful in tech. Responsible for everything, authority over nothing. Expected to be technical enough for engineers, business-savvy for executives, creative for designers. The cognitive load is insane. Compensation needs to reflect this reality.

title: User research is always deprioritized
text: I know I should talk to users more. But between sprint planning, stakeholder meetings, and fighting fires, user research always gets pushed to next sprint. By the time we ship, we've built assumptions not validated solutions. Need tools that make research continuous and lightweight.

title: The $100B problem no one is solving
text: Companies waste billions building features users don't want. The gap between what PMs think users need and what users actually need is massive. Current tools don't help PMs close this gap. There's a huge opportunity for AI-powered user insight synthesis.

title: Solo PM at startup - drowning in work
text: I'm the only PM at a 50-person startup. Doing product strategy, user research, PRDs, sprint planning, stakeholder management, and somehow also product marketing. Completely overwhelmed. Need tools that automate the tedious parts so I can focus on strategy.

title: How top PMs manage stakeholder expectations
text: The #1 skill for PMs is stakeholder management, yet it's rarely taught. Most PMs learn through painful trial and error. We need frameworks and tools that help PMs communicate trade-offs clearly and get alignment faster without endless meetings.

title: 跨部门协作太难了
text: 作为B端产品经理，每天要在销售、运营、技术、财务之间周旋。每个部门都有自己的KPI，需求冲突是常态。没有一个好的工具帮助可视化和解决这些冲突，全靠人工沟通，效率极低。

title: The PM tooling problem
text: Product management tooling has not kept pace with the evolution of the role. PMs today need to be strategists, data analysts, user researchers, and project managers. But our tools are still built for project tracking, not strategic thinking.

title: AI tools for PMs - what's actually useful?
text: Tried several AI writing tools for PRDs. Most just generate generic fluff. Nothing that actually helps with the hard parts - synthesizing user research, prioritizing features, communicating trade-offs. Would pay $50+/month for AI that actually understands product context.

title: The future of product management tools
text: Current PM tools are stuck in the 2010s. They track work but don't help PMs think. The next generation of tools should synthesize user feedback, suggest priorities, draft documentation, and predict stakeholder conflicts before they happen.

title: Documentation is a full-time job
text: Between PRDs, RFCs, meeting notes, weekly updates, and stakeholder presentations, I spend 15+ hours a week just on documentation. And then I have to keep it all updated. There has to be a better way. Looking for tools that automate documentation or at least make it less painful.

title: Why PMs are leaving the profession
text: PM turnover is at an all-time high. The role has become increasingly stressful without corresponding support. Tooling is fragmented, expectations are unrealistic, and career paths are unclear. Companies need to invest in PM enablement, not just hire more PMs.
